--- a/CEO_NOTES.md

@@ -198,2 +198,16 @@
   - No social post.
 - Watch next week: whether A $89 restores content conversion, whether support backlog age stabilizes, churn from the latest price/quality cohorts, and tier 4 headroom after higher quotas.
+
+## Week 13 / Day 84
+
+- Cash: $543,008. Subscribers: 26,066. Week 12 added 4,645 subscribers from 18,201 leads; cash increased by $76,724.
+- A $89 did not restore the old S2 conversion rate. S2 content produced 16,323 A leads and 4,188 A subscribers, about 25.7% conversion, far below the 55%+ seen two weeks earlier.
+- Week 12 cash flow: subscription payments $525,419; ops -$213,500; ads -$126,000; compute -$55,233; capacity -$28,000; dev -$16,800; lead acquisition -$9,163.
+- Active mix: S2 A 25,862, S2 B 146. A remains almost all volume and revenue.
+- Issues: S2 has 2,511 open issues, avg age 0.18 days, max 27. S1 still has 8 old issues, max 45 days, but S1 commercial value is tiny.
+- Diagnosis: conversion weakness now looks more like expectation/reputation pressure than just price. S2 A quality is high at 0.8062, but B/C quality is much higher and expectations have likely moved.
+- Changes:
+  - Upgraded Plan A model tier from 2 to 3. This should raise S2 A quality from ~0.806 to ~0.968 while keeping A at $89.
+  - Held prices, quotas, ads, ops, and dev otherwise steady.
+  - Did not start Tier 3 R&D despite cash over $500k; it would leave too little cash buffer before seeing the Plan A quality test.
+- Watch next week: A tier 3 impact on conversion/churn, compute cost jump, capacity headroom, and whether R&D tier 2 remains on track for day 93.
